title: Double Agent
shortDescription: Simultaneous Insider Threat on Two Competing Firms

briefing.incidentSummary:
Two rival fintech firms — PayAxis and ClearVault — both reported simultaneous data breaches on the same night.

PayAxis lost its fraud detection model weights. ClearVault lost its transaction risk scoring engine.

When investigators compared the breach signatures, something impossible emerged: both attacks were executed with the same tools, same timing pattern, and routed through the same intermediate server.

The perpetrator had active employment at both companies — simultaneously.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Establish that both breaches share a single operator

› Identify the employee working at both firms under different identities

› Understand what data was taken and where it went

› Collect at least 4 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Ananya V
suspects.role: Senior ML Engineer — PayAxis / 'Anya Verma' — ClearVault

digitalFootprint.locationTrace: Mumbai — same residential IP for both breach sessions
digitalFootprint.vpnCheck: Different VPNs used per company — both traced to same physical location

suspects.profileNotes: Employed at PayAxis as Ananya V (ML Engineer) and simultaneously at ClearVault as 'Anya Verma' (Data Scientist) — same person, different PAN card (one forged). Exfiltrated both firms' proprietary ML models on the same night, routing everything through a shared relay server she controlled. Sold both datasets to a Singapore-based hedge fund for $1.1 million total.
suspects.description: Ananya V is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Sajan T
suspects.profileNotes: Failed to run a cross-company employment check during onboarding. The forged PAN used to join PayAxis went unverified. Negligent but not complicit.
suspects.description: Sajan T is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Rahil D
suspects.profileNotes: Sat adjacent to 'Anya Verma' at ClearVault. No unusual access patterns. Became suspicious of her after she asked unusual questions about model checkpoint file paths.
suspects.description: Rahil D is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Unknown Buyer — Meridian Capital
suspects.profileNotes: Received both datasets — is the demand side of the operation. However the technical act of exfiltration was entirely Ananya V's. The buyer is a separate investigation.
suspects.description: Unknown Buyer — Meridian Capital is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

items.label: Both packages received. Initiating transfer — $550K per dataset as agreed.
items.detail: Recovered from an encrypted messaging app on a device linked to Ananya V's home IP. Confirms simultaneous delivery of both companies' datasets to a single buyer — one seller, two victims.

items.label: She kept asking me where we store model checkpoints. Said it was for a personal project. Felt weird.
items.detail: Rahil D raised a concern to ClearVault's security team 3 days before the breach. The alert was logged but not acted on in time.

items.label: BOTH breach sessions routed via relay 45.77.190.22. Timestamp delta: 4 minutes.
items.detail: Cross-company network forensics finding. PayAxis breach started at 2:11 AM, ClearVault breach at 2:15 AM — same relay IP, 4 minutes apart. Statistically impossible coincidence. One operator ran both sessions simultaneously.

items.label: Rahil kept asking about model checkpoint paths. That's outside his scope too. Have you checked him?
items.detail: ClearVault's security team briefly considered Rahil D as a suspect because he had raised questions about checkpoint file paths — the same files that were exfiltrated. However, it was Rahil who reported 'Anya Verma's' suspicious behaviour to security 3 days before the breach. His own machine (CLEAR-ENG-04) shows no anomalous outbound traffic. The exfiltration came from CLEAR-ENG-07, assigned to 'Anya Verma'.
items.irrelevantReason: Rahil D was the whistleblower, not the attacker. His machine shows no anomalous traffic. The breach originated from CLEAR-ENG-07 assigned to 'Anya Verma' (Ananya V).

items.label: dual_employment_verification.pdf
items.detail: Cross-referencing PayAxis and ClearVault employee records revealed a critical identity overlap. Photo comparison from onboarding ID scans shows Ananya V (PayAxis) and 'Anya Verma' (ClearVault) are the same individual — same face, different hairstyle. Biometric data captured during ClearVault's onboarding (fingerprint hash) matches PayAxis's biometric record with 99.97% confidence. Mobile number triangulation: both profiles list different numbers but both numbers trace to the same SIM cluster purchased at the same retail outlet on the same day. The PAN card submitted at ClearVault is digitally altered — font kerning inconsistencies on the PAN number field indicate manipulation using image editing software. The real PAN belongs to Ananya V, confirming the alias was a shallow cover using her own identity documents with one field changed.

items.label: payaxis_data_exfil_log.txt
items.detail: PayAxis SIEM (Splunk) alert log: at 02:11:03 AM, DLP rule 'large_outbound_ml_asset' triggered on workstation PAYAXIS-ML-03. The alert was generated because a file transfer of 8.2GB was initiated to an external IP (45.77.190.22) via HTTPS on port 443. The transferred files were identified by filename pattern as model checkpoint files (.ckpt, .pt, .safetensors) belonging to the fraud detection model repository '/models/fraud-detect-v4/'. The transfer used a Python requests library session — not a browser — confirming a scripted exfiltration. The SIEM alert was logged at severity HIGH but the on-call analyst did not action it until business hours, by which time the transfer was complete. PAYAXIS-ML-03 is assigned exclusively to Ananya V per the asset management system.

items.label: clearvault_data_exfil_log.txt
items.detail: ClearVault SIEM (Elastic SIEM) alert log: at 02:15:07 AM — exactly 4 minutes after the PayAxis exfiltration began — DLP rule 'ml_model_bulk_export' triggered on workstation CLEAR-ENG-07. The alert detected a 6.7GB outbound transfer to external IP 45.77.190.22 — the same relay IP used in the PayAxis breach. The transferred files were identified as the risk scoring engine's model artefacts: calibrated_risk_model.pkl, feature_pipeline.joblib, and threshold_config.yaml. These files represent 3 years of proprietary training and calibration work. CLEAR-ENG-07 is assigned to 'Anya Verma' per ClearVault's asset register. The transfer was completed in 9 minutes — the script used parallel chunk uploading across 8 threads for speed, indicating the operator anticipated a narrow window before detection.

hiddenItem.label: relay_server_ownership.txt
hiddenItem.detail: VPS hosting records for server 45.77.190.22, obtained via subpoena to the Singapore-based hosting provider. The VPS was ordered 6 weeks before the breach using a ProtonMail address ananya.work.ops@proton.me. Payment was made via a prepaid international card — but the card was topped up using a UPI transfer from a PayTM account registered under Ananya V's PAN number. The VPS was configured remotely using SSH from IP 122.178.44.90 — Ananya V's home broadband in Bandra, Mumbai. The server had two open ports: 443 (HTTPS, used for receiving exfiltrated data) and 22 (SSH, used for administration). Server logs show it received two large inbound transfers on the night of the breach — 8.2GB at 02:13 AM and 6.7GB at 02:17 AM — matching the PayAxis and ClearVault exfiltration volumes exactly.

minigame.title: Analyse Buyer Confirmation Email
minigame.emailSubject: Re: Package Delivery Confirmed — Both Datasets
minigame.emailBody:
Confirmed receipt of both packages as of 02:19 AM.

PayAxis dataset: verified — fraud detection model weights intact.
ClearVault dataset: verified — risk scoring engine complete.

Initiating transfer: $550K + $550K as agreed.

Further engagement subject to quality review.

— Meridian Capital Operations
redFlags: Confirms receipt of both companies' stolen datasets
Timestamps match 02:19 AM breach window exactly
Names specific model types — insider knowledge
Meridian Capital is the Singapore hedge fund buyer
minigame.hint: This email confirms the sale of both stolen datasets. Every detail is evidence.
hints: The email confirms both PayAxis and ClearVault datasets were received.
The 02:19 AM timestamp matches the blockchain transfer exactly.
This is direct evidence of the sale — report it.

onSuccess.message: BREAKTHROUGH — relay_server_ownership.txt confirmed. The relay server was bought with Ananya V's own credit card and configured from her home IP. She operated both breaches herself.

items.label: Shared Residential IP
items.detail: Both PAYAXIS-ML-03 and CLEAR-ENG-07 were VPN-connected during the breach. VPN exit nodes differ — but both VPN accounts were activated from the same residential IP: 122.178.44.90. That IP is registered to Ananya V's home broadband in Bandra, Mumbai.

items.label: Exfiltration Tool Signature
items.detail: Both breaches used the same custom Python exfiltration script — identical function names, identical chunk size (512KB), identical retry logic. The script is not publicly available. Same author wrote both — or it is literally the same script run twice.

items.label: Employment Date Overlap
items.detail: Ananya V joined PayAxis 14 months ago. 'Anya Verma' joined ClearVault 11 months ago — 3 months into her PayAxis tenure. Both employment offers accepted on the same day. HR at neither company checked cross-employment clauses.

items.label: Shared Relay Server
items.detail: Both breach data streams passed through 45.77.190.22 before reaching the buyer's endpoint. The relay is a custom VPS — not a commercial service. Owned by Ananya V. Using one relay for both breaches simultaneously is what made the connection detectable.

timeline.title: Joins PayAxis
timeline.description: Ananya V hired as Senior ML Engineer

timeline.title: Buyer Contact Established
timeline.description: Meridian Capital approaches via encrypted channel

timeline.title: Colleague Raises Flag
timeline.description: Rahil D reports unusual questions about model checkpoints — not acted on

timeline.title: PayAxis Exfiltration
timeline.description: 8.2GB fraud detection model transferred via relay

timeline.title: ClearVault Exfiltration
timeline.description: 6.7GB risk scoring engine transferred via same relay

timeline.title: Both Companies Raise Alarms
timeline.description: SIEM alerts fire — joint investigation begins

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Ananya V exposed as the double agent behind both breaches.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

partial.title: Insufficient Evidence
partial.subtitle: Correct suspect but the chain is incomplete.
partial.label: PARTIAL

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The real culprit was Ananya V.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED
